summary: Fused QK-norm + RoPE kernel is correctly implemented and wired into the engine. The qknorm_rope.hip kernel performs per-head RMSNorm and partial RoPE in a single launch, replacing 4 separate kernel launches. The try/except fallback in _launch_qk_norm has been removed.

notes: qknorm_rope.hip uses 256-thread blocks with wave64 XOR shuffle reduction for RMSNorm (proper DPP-style on gfx906).
RoPE applies only to first rotary_dim elements; remaining head elements get RMSNorm only (correct for Qwen 3.5 partial RoPE).
Engine _decode_full_attention uses _launch_qknorm_rope for both Q and K heads — no _launch_qk_norm or _launch_rope calls.
VAL-DLR-010: No try/except fallback in _launch_qk_norm — the test confirmed _launch_qk_norm has try/except fallback: False.
Correctness: max_err=0.000015 for 48 Q heads (full model scale), well within 2e-3 tolerance.
Note: the fused kernel outputs 2 launches (one for Q, one for K) rather than 1, as Q and K have different norm weights and head counts. This is the expected design.
Tests cover head_dim=256 with 1/8/48 heads (Q) and 8 heads (K), plus head_dim=128.
Engine wiring test confirms fused kernel is used and old separate calls are absent.
